lastUpdatedNote: PR #55 (Phase 6 PR 1C — tier-gated template list + Hi-Fi upgrade page) merged. Locked-card upsell UX live: Free users see all 13 templates in the v2 Playbook, 8 of them dimmed with 'Upgrade to All-in' ghost button routing to a new /upgrade.html comparison page. PR 1A (mechanism) and PR 1B (curated split) already shipped. Tier-gate is now enforced AND visible end-to-end. Phase 6 sprint active — next: day-zero auto-activation.

sprint.goal: Effective implementation of Hi-Fi v2, with ROSS as the central post-login surface for every subscribed user
sprint.summary: Concierge-first IA. v1 admin tabs reframed as 'Ross's playbook' — the rules and procedures the future AI agent runs against. Phase 5 closed 2026-05-11 with all five cleanup PRs merged (#39 / #42 / #44 / #46 / #48): ROSS is now the post-login destination end-to-end; homepage / signup / sidebar all Hi-Fi-promoted. Phase 6 active: Playbook UX enrichment + template library curation. PR 1A (the mechanism) shipped 2026-05-12; next is PR 1B (curation — flip some templates to All-in). Workflows are the product; v1 modules become workflow step types over time. Two tiers only: Free (full UI + 5 starter templates), All-in (full library).

phases.name: ROSS v2 — gap analysis & spec
phases.summary: PR #18. Audit found home feed already wired behind ROSS_HOME_REAL_DATA flag.

phases.name: ROSS v2 — wire right-rail + first-run findings
ROSS v2 — action handlers + snooze
phases.summary: PR #20. dispatch(action, card) + rossV2Snooze CF deployed + read-side filter.

phases.name: ROSS v2 — admin redesign (concierge-first IA)
phases.summary: All Phase 4 tasks merged: 4a Playbook (PRs #21/#24), 4b Activity (#23), 4c People CRUD (#25), 4d.1 Workflow editor (#28), 4d.2 Template CRUD superAdmin (#30), 4e.1 Per-task input-type editor (#32), 4e.2 Subtask→task propagation + template-level inputType editor (#35). All v2 admin tabs are flag-ON behind /ross.html and edit-capable end-to-end.

phases.name: ROSS v2 — central-funnel cleanup
phases.summary: Closed 2026-05-11. Five PRs all merged: post-login router (#39), signup v2 Hi-Fi + dynamic tier writes (#42), homepage v2 Hi-Fi + reused public hello (#44), router rollout + drop wizard for fresh signups (#46, PR 4 + PR 6 folded), ROSS sidebar cleanup (#48). ROSS is now the post-login destination end-to-end: fresh signup → hello → ross; existing signed-in users → ross; sidebar collapsed to Today + Ross's brain; module deep-links removed from ROSS primary nav. Q1–Q5 product questions resolved: Free = full UI + 5 starter templates, day-zero = pre-populated demo workflow, v1 access = admin-only via admin-dashboard, hello = same component two data feeds, ROSS_IS_HOME flag wired for rollback safety.

phases.name: ROSS v2 — playbook UX enrichment + template library curation
phases.summary: Active sprint. PR 1A (#51, 2026-05-12) shipped the tier-gating mechanism. PR 1B (#53, 2026-05-12) curated the 5 Free / 8 All-in split. PR 1C (#55, 2026-05-12) shipped the locked-card upsell UX in the v2 Playbook tab + Hi-Fi /upgrade.html comparison page (email CTA to andreas@sparksclub.co.za, D-ready for self-service checkout). Tier-gate is now enforced AND visible. Next: day-zero auto-activation (seed one starter workflow per new account), then operator-facing Run execution UI for all 10 inputTypes, then concierge home cards driven by real activated workflows. The Runs server surface is already shipped. Vocabulary per public/kb/features/ROSS.md: Templates → Workflows → Tasks → Runs.

phases.name: ROSS v2 — askRoss (LLM)
phases.summary: Separate sprint. Anthropic-backed rossChat CF + eval harness + cost cap + web-sourced day-zero findings (weather, local events, holidays, suburb demographics).

bugs.title: feature-access-control.js:64 — dynamic import with ?v=${Date.now()} cache-buster breaks Vite build-time analysis
bugs.notes: Vite can't statically analyse a runtime-computed path — throws `Unknown variable dynamic import` on every checkFeatureAccess call post-build. Fix is one line (drop the ?v=…; Vite content-hashes outputs natively) but the file is shared infra. Needs own focused PR with consumer audit.
